name: claude-code-diff-review
description: Per-hunk review of Claude Code session changes with in-context AI chat. Pro/Max OAuth and API-key auth supported.

commands.title: Claude Review: Switch Install Scope (user ↔ workspace)
Claude Review: Set Anthropic API Key
Claude Review: Clear Anthropic API Key
Claude Review: Set Claude OAuth Token (Max plan)
Claude Review: Clear Claude OAuth Token
Claude Review: Use Claude Code Auth (probe & report)

claudeReview.installScope.description: Where to install Claude Code hooks. `user` (default) writes to ~/.claude/settings.json so every project picks them up automatically. `workspace` writes to <workspace>/.claude/settings.json — per-project. Use the 'Switch Install Scope' command to toggle.

claudeReview.dualScope.allow.description: Power-user escape hatch. When false (default), the extension auto-removes hook entries from the inactive scope if both ~/.claude/settings.json and <workspace>/.claude/settings.json carry our marker — prevents the dual-instance state where Claude's hook precedence orphans one extension. Set true to deliberately keep both scopes active (e.g., for multi-version testing); a warning toast surfaces on activation when both are present.

claudeReview.history.enabled.description: Persist every Claude Code turn (started, stopped, hunk decisions) to a content-addressed event log. Powers the History panel and crash recovery. Disable to opt out entirely; existing logs are not deleted.

claudeReview.history.retentionDays.description: Number of days to retain Memory Design event log sessions. The retention sweeper removes older sessions + their unreferenced blobs every 10 minutes.

claudeReview.crashRecoveryToast.enabled.description: Deprecated. Off by default in v0.4 — the status-bar pending-reviews indicator covers a strict superset of cases. Set true to re-enable the activation toast for one more release; this setting will be removed in v0.5.

claudeReview.chat.transcriptContext.description: Inject Claude Code's transcript context (the user's original prompt + Claude's surrounding tool calls) into hunk chat queries. Off → chat sees only the hunk diff. Transcript content is read host-side only and never forwarded to the webview.

claudeReview.riskFlags.enabled.description: Surface heuristic-based risk flags on files and hunks (sensitive paths like .env / migrations / auth, pure deletions, removed error handling, removed null checks, large hunks, lockfiles, test files). Flags help prioritise which hunks to review most carefully — useful for sessions with 20+ edits. Off → no flag chips render.

claudeReview.buildSignal.enabled.description: v0.5 build signal — run the workspace's TypeScript compiler (tsc --noEmit) after Claude finishes a turn and annotate each file + hunk with whether it affects a failing typecheck. The session-header banner shows aggregate status (running/passed/failed); affected files get a red dot in the sidebar; affected hunks get an inline 🚨 badge. Off → no spawn, no banner, no annotations. Currently supports TypeScript only; jest/vitest/pytest/cargo/go support arrives in v0.6.

claudeReview.buildSignal.typecheckComman.description: v0.5 build signal — override the auto-detected tsc invocation. Example: `tsc -b --noEmit -p apps/web`. Leave blank to auto-detect: `tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json` for single-tsconfig repos; `tsc -b --noEmit` when `composite: true` or `references[]` is present. The string is tokenised respecting quotes; arguments are passed via argv, NOT through a shell — no shell injection vector even if your workspace path contains backticks or `&`.

claudeReview.buildSignal.timeoutMs.description: v0.5 build signal — wall-clock timeout for the typecheck subprocess in milliseconds. On timeout, the process tree is force-killed (taskkill on Windows, SIGTERM on POSIX); affected files surface as buildStatus: 'unknown' with a banner indication. Distinct from cancel-on-next-Stop. 120 s default; raise on slow monorepos.

claudeReview.autoOpenPanel.description: Open the review panel automatically when Claude Code finishes a turn.

claudeReview.chatModel.description: Anthropic model used for hunk chat. Haiku is fast and cheap and a good fit for explaining a single hunk; switch to a larger model (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-7) for deeper reviews.

claudeReview.chatMaxTokens.description: Maximum output tokens per chat response.

claudeReview.maxSessionBytes.description: Maximum bytes of original-file snapshots retained per session.

claudeReview.telemetry.description: Opt in to anonymous usage telemetry. Also gated by VS Code's global telemetry setting.

scripts.release:check: npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test && npm run audit:licenses && npm run build
